stem: Evaluate \( \left\lfloor \dfrac{[[p]]}{[[q]]} \right\rfloor \), where \( \lfloor \cdot \rfloor \) denotes the greatest integer less than or equal to its argument.
solution: \( \dfrac{[[p]]}{[[q]]} \) is not an integer, so it lies strictly between two consecutive integers. The greatest integer not exceeding it is \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This rounds up toward \( +\infty \) (or truncates toward zero for a negative value), giving the ceiling instead of the floor.
This is one less than the greatest integer that fits.
This is two more than the correct floor.
This is two less than the correct floor.
This is three more than the correct floor.

stem: Evaluate \( \left\lceil \dfrac{[[p]]}{[[q]]} \right\rceil \), where \( \lceil \cdot \rceil \) denotes the least integer greater than or equal to its argument.
solution: \( \dfrac{[[p]]}{[[q]]} \) is not an integer, so it lies strictly between two consecutive integers. The least integer not smaller than it is \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This rounds down instead of up, giving the floor instead of the ceiling.
This is one less than the correct ceiling.
This is two more than the correct ceiling.
This is two less than the correct ceiling.
This is three more than the correct ceiling.

solution: For any \( x \) that is not an integer, \( \lfloor x \rfloor \) and \( \lfloor -x \rfloor \) are \( n \) and \( -n-1 \) for the integer \( n = \lfloor x \rfloor \), so their sum is always \( -1 \). Here \( \lfloor x \rfloor + \lfloor -x \rfloor = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This assumes \( \lfloor -x \rfloor = -\lfloor x \rfloor \), which only holds when \( x \) is an integer.
This assumes the two floors partially cancel to a small positive number rather than always giving \( -1 \).
This doubles \( \lfloor x \rfloor \) as if \( \lfloor -x \rfloor = \lfloor x \rfloor \), ignoring the sign flip.
This is off by one from the correct (constant) value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: What is the smallest real number \( x \) for which \( \left\lfloor \dfrac{x - ([[a]])}{[[b]]} \right\rfloor = [[n]] \)?
solution: \( \left\lfloor \frac{x-([[a]])}{[[b]]} \right\rfloor = [[n]] \) exactly when \( [[n]] \le \frac{x-([[a]])}{[[b]]} < [[n]]+1 \), i.e. \( [[a]] + [[n]]\cdot[[b]] \le x < [[a]] + ([[n]]+1)\cdot[[b]] \). The smallest such \( x \) is the left endpoint, \( x = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This uses the right endpoint of the interval, which is excluded (the interval is closed only on the left).
This flips the sign when solving for the endpoint.
This multiplies \( a \) and \( n \) instead of scaling \( n \) by \( b \) and adding \( a \).
This forgets to add \( [[a]] \), the horizontal shift.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: How many integers \( n \) with \( 1 \le n \le [[N]] \) satisfy \( \left\lfloor \dfrac{n}{[[a]]} \right\rfloor = [[k]] \)?
solution: \( \lfloor n/[[a]] \rfloor = [[k]] \) exactly when \( [[a]]\cdot[[k]] \le n \le [[a]]\cdot([[k]]+1)-1 \). Intersecting this range with \( 1 \le n \le [[N]] \) and counting the integers gives \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This measures from \( [[a]]\cdot[[k]] \) to \( [[N]] \) but forgets to add 1 when counting an inclusive range of integers.
This measures from \( [[a]]\cdot[[k]] \) to \( [[N]] \) directly, forgetting to also cap at the upper end of the block \( [[a]]\cdot([[k]]+1)-1 \) when it is smaller than \( [[N]] \).
This is off by one from the correct count.
This is off by one from the correct count.
This is off by two from the correct count.
This is off by two from the correct count.

solution: Find the two consecutive perfect squares that bracket [[N]]: \( [[answer]]^{2} \le [[N]] < ([[answer]]+1)^{2} \). So \( \left\lfloor \sqrt{[[N]]} \right\rfloor = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This rounds up to the next integer instead of down, giving the ceiling instead of the floor.
This overshoots to the next integer above the correct floor.
This undershoots to the integer below the correct floor.
This is off by two above the correct floor.
This is off by two below the correct floor.
This halves \( [[N]] \) instead of taking its square root.
